title: next surfaces an open arc root whose members are still open — make rollup structural
body:
Reported from agent fleets on Y and FactoryGame: a root filed with trk in / add --in appears on trk next while its members are open, because next only consults needs edges and never checks isArc on the candidate itself. Eight hand-repaired rollup edges accumulated in under a week.

Agreed direction (pending design discussion): fix eligibility in Store.next — an open task that isArc is eligible only when no direct non-parked member fails satisfiesPrereq (vacuously eligible when all members are parked; do NOT reuse arcComplete, which returns false for that case). Do the same in render so the projection agrees.

Rejected: auto-creating root-needs-member edges at authoring time — cycle risk with arc-as-prereq (EXIT-threshold pattern: E needs arc A, member M needs E, auto-edge A->M closes the cycle), log noise, needs a paired auto-undep, and does not repair existing trackers.

title: show --body: raw body read path for safe edit round-trips
body: trk show indents the body for display and there was no raw accessor, so editing a body meant parsing log.jsonl by hand or round-tripping indented display output (caused the 0d2 body corruption). Added: trk show <id> --body prints the body verbatim, making trk edit <id> --body "$(trk show <id> --body)" lossless.

title: doc unset: tombstone a doc_id -> path registration
body: No way to unregister a doc mapping (ProgramPassAudit -> blank). Added: trk doc unset <doc_id> emits setDocPath with an empty path; the fold removes the mapping (resolve fails, list hides, render falls back to the raw doc_id), serializeState never emits removed entries so compact GCs the tombstone. Idempotent like untag/undep; a later set revives (last-write-wins).

Reported from agent fleets on Y and FactoryGame: a root filed with trk in / add --in appears on trk next while its members are open, because next only consults needs edges and never checks isArc on the candidate itself. Eight hand-repaired rollup edges accumulated in under a week.

Agreed direction (pending design discussion): fix eligibility in Store.next — an open task that isArc is eligible only when no direct non-parked member fails satisfiesPrereq (vacuously eligible when all members are parked; do NOT reuse arcComplete, which returns false for that case). Do the same in render so the projection agrees.

Rejected: auto-creating root-needs-member edges at authoring time — cycle risk with arc-as-prereq (EXIT-threshold pattern: E needs arc A, member M needs E, auto-edge A->M closes the cycle), log noise, needs a paired auto-undep, and does not repair existing trackers.

Fixed via the drained-vs-complete ruling (2026-07-13), red-teamed before landing. An arc root is a container: Store.next holds an open root back until the arc is drained (arcDrained: no direct non-parked member unsatisfied; vacuously drained when all members are parked), then surfaces it once as the close-out prompt. The arc-as-prereq special case was deleted: a needs edge targeting a root gates on the root's own state — the explicit completion judgment — so closing the root is what unblocks dependents. Rejected: authoring-time derived root-needs-member edges (union-merge can forge a cycle that bricks the store; ignores parked; irreversible) and a done-when-complete auto-close field (conflates the observable drained fact with the completion judgment; auto-done + archive graduates a still-growing arc into an immutable tombstone). Full rationale in docs/design.md.
